skill.name: sending-emails
skill.description: Guides Mailtrap live email sending through Email API, SMTP, batch requests, bulk streams, and official SDK choices.
skill.summary: Configure and troubleshoot Mailtrap live email sending with API, SMTP, batch, bulk, and SDK guidance.
skill.author: mailtrap

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution detections are false positives from Markdown code spans and curl examples, not executable skill code. The real risk is legitimate live email sending through Mailtrap API and SMTP endpoints, which can send outbound mail when a user supplies credentials. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious endpoint, or hidden execution behavior was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Live Email Sending Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Mailtrap Email API and SMTP instructions for sending live outbound email. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can send real messages when a user supplies a valid token.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly document live API and SMTP endpoints with curl examples. The behavior is intentional and legitimate, but it has clear outbound-email impact.

medium_findings.title: API Token Used In Sending Examples
medium_findings.description: The examples reference MAILTRAP_API_TOKEN in authorization headers and SMTP password fields. No secret is hardcoded, but users must handle the token carefully to avoid disclosure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token appears only as an environment variable placeholder, which is safer than a literal secret. The risk remains meaningful because copied commands use the token for live sending.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Official Mailtrap Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill contains fixed Mailtrap API, SMTP, documentation, and SDK URLs. These are expected for an integration guide and point to Mailtrap-owned service paths, not suspicious third-party collection endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains and links match the stated Mailtrap sending purpose. I found no evidence of hidden or unrelated outbound destinations.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Command Execution Detections
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many Markdown backticks and shell examples as Ruby or shell execution. SKILL.md is documentation, and no executable script file or automatic command runner is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged material is inside Markdown prose or fenced bash examples. There is no evidence that the skill executes these commands by itself.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Scanner Blocker False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts do not match the cited lines. Line 3 is frontmatter description syntax, and line 30 discusses Mailtrap Campaigns product selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no cryptographic algorithm and no system reconnaissance command. These are static pattern errors, not confirmed security issues.

dangerous_patterns.title: Curl Requests To Live Sending APIs
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation includes curl examples that post to live Mailtrap sending endpoints. Users should verify recipients, stream type, and test strategy before running adapted commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit POST requests to live send or batch endpoints. They are not malicious, but they are operationally sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: SMTP Configuration For Live Delivery
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents live and bulk SMTP hosts with an API token as the password. Misuse could send mail from the wrong stream or expose credentials in client configuration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly document live SMTP hosts and token-based authentication. This is expected Mailtrap guidance, but credential handling and stream selection require care.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear choices between Mailtrap API, SMTP, batch, bulk, and SDK integrations. This skill guides setup, troubleshooting, and safe live sending decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Mailtrap platform integrations, SDKs, HTTP API, or SMTP.
Distinguishes transactional, bulk, batch, and Campaigns sending paths.
Provides endpoint, host, port, and token placement guidance for live sending.
Shows how template UUIDs and variables fit Mailtrap API requests.
Lists common mistakes for stream selection, SMTP login, and domain readiness.
limitations: It does not replace Mailtrap account setup or sending domain verification.
It does not provide a full API reference for every optional request field.
It does not manage recipient consent, suppression policy, or legal compliance.
SDK method names must be checked against current official SDK README files.

use_cases.title: Add Transactional Sending
use_cases.description: Implement password reset, receipt, alert, or notification email through the correct Mailtrap live sending path.

use_cases.title: Choose Bulk Or Campaigns
use_cases.description: Decide whether promotional mail should use the bulk stream, batch endpoint, or Mailtrap Campaigns product.

use_cases.title: Debug Sending Configuration
use_cases.description: Check hosts, endpoints, token placement, SMTP credentials, and common stream selection mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Choose A Sending Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose between Mailtrap platform integration, SDK, Email API, and SMTP for my application. Ask for missing context first.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Transactional Send Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Mailtrap transactional email implementation plan for password reset messages. Include token handling, domain readiness, and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Bulk Sending Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Mailtrap bulk email approach. Check whether I should use bulk API, batch, or Campaigns, and identify compliance risks.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot Failed Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this Mailtrap sending failure using endpoint, stream, SMTP host, token scope, domain verification, and suppression checks.

output_examples.input: I need to send password reset messages from a Node app.
output_examples.output: Recommended path: use the official SDK or Email API on the transactional stream. Verify the sending domain, store the token in an environment variable, and test before live recipients.

output_examples.input: We have 300 promotional messages ready to send.
output_examples.output: Use the bulk stream for promotional content. Use batch only to submit many messages together. Consider Campaigns when Mailtrap should manage contacts and reporting.

output_examples.input: SMTP login fails with my email address as username.
output_examples.output: Use api as the SMTP username and the Mailtrap API token as the password. Confirm you selected the live or bulk SMTP host.

best_practices: Prefer platform integrations or official SDKs before direct HTTP or SMTP implementations.
Store Mailtrap API tokens in environment variables and rotate them when exposed.
Verify sending domains, recipient type, stream, and suppression behavior before live sends.
anti_patterns: Do not use the transactional host for promotional or bulk stream traffic.
Do not treat batch as a third stream; it is a request format for many messages.
Do not guess SDK constructors or methods without checking the current SDK README.

faq.question: Can this skill send real email?
faq.answer: It can guide real sending through Mailtrap. Actual delivery requires your Mailtrap account, verified domain, recipients, and valid token.

faq.question: Should I use SMTP or the Email API?
faq.answer: Prefer platform integrations, then official SDKs, then HTTP API. Use SMTP when your platform requires it.

faq.question: What is the difference between bulk and batch?
faq.answer: Bulk is the promotional sending stream. Batch is a way to submit many messages in one API request.

faq.question: Does this cover Mailtrap Campaigns setup?
faq.answer: It explains when Campaigns are a better path. It does not replace the Mailtrap UI setup workflow.

faq.question: Are API tokens included in the skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses token placeholders and expects users to provide and protect their own credentials.

faq.question: Can I use templates with this guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains template UUIDs and variables, and points users to template-specific guidance for more detail.
